events.title: Into the Woods
events.description: Back to Event Listing // If the user came from a different site or has referrer clicking the back button will take them to the default listing page if (document.referrer === '' || document.referrer.search('du.edu') === -1) { document.querySelector('.sub-menu__back-link').onclick = function(e) { window.location.href = 'https://www.du.edu/calendar'; } } March 8 2:00pm - 4:30pm March 7 7:30pm - 10:00pm March 6 7:30pm - 10:00pm March 5 7:30pm - 10:00pm February 28 7:30pm - 10:00pm February 27 7:30pm - 10:00pm February 26 7:30pm - 10:00pm Into the Woods takes us beyond "happily ever after" to ask: What do you do when you get what you wished for? In Stephen Sondheim's beloved musical, familiar fairy tale characters cross paths as their dreams come true-only to discover that life after the wish is far more complicated. Into the Woods explores the consequences of desire, the cost of choice, and the unexpected strength we find in each other when the story takes a darker turn. Music and lyrics by Stephen SondheimBook by James LapineDirected by Kelly Van OosbreePurchase tickets onlineVisitor parking is available in the Newman Center Garage, located off Wesley Ave. and University Blvd. The cost is $2/hour.Free street parking is also available in the neighborhood surrounding the Newman Center.There is no late seating.

events.title: Masterclass: Nermis Mieses, oboe
events.description: Back to Event Listing // If the user came from a different site or has referrer clicking the back button will take them to the default listing page if (document.referrer === '' || document.referrer.search('du.edu') === -1) { document.querySelector('.sub-menu__back-link').onclick = function(e) { window.location.href = 'https://www.du.edu/calendar'; } } March 6 12:00pm - 1:00pm Newman Center for the Performing Arts, Room 209, Classroom Free admission to observeThis event will not be livestreamedNermis Mieses is the Associate Professor of Oboe at Michigan State University and faculty at the Sewanee Summer Music Festival. She has received prizes at the prestigious Barbirolli International Oboe Competition, the First International Oboe Competition in Santa Catarina, Brazil, and the Matthew Ruggiero International Woodwind Competition.As part of her research interests, she continues to champion repertoire from the iconic French oboist-composer, Gilles Silvestrini, as well as music from underrepresented composers. She is frequently featured in universities, conservatories, chamber music series, and as a presenter at the IDRS conferences.Equally dedicated to ensemble playing, she spent nine seasons as Principal Oboe of the Michigan Opera Theatre and has collaborated with symphony orchestras such as Detroit, Ann Arbor, Sphinx, ProMusica Chamber, and the Michigan Philharmonic. Internationally, she has performed with Camerata Colonial in the Dominican Republic, the Puerto Rico Symphony Orchestra, the Thy Chamber Music Festival in Denmark, and the Chineke! Orchestra in Europe.Passionate about her oboe studio, Dr. Mieses is known for her nurturing, yet ambitious teaching style. Excited about mentoring, she guides her students to achieve healthy growth as learners, instrumentalists, musicians, and artists. Previously, she taught at Bowling Green State University, the University of Kentucky, Ohio Northern, and Hillsdale College. She holds DMA and MM degrees from the University of Michigan under Dr. Nancy Ambrose King, where she was conferred the 2020 Paul Boyland Award by the School of Music Theatre and Dance Alumni Society of Governors. She is a proud mother of two children and frequently performs with her husband, pianist Xavier Suarez. For ticket questions, please contact the Newman Center Box Office at 303-871-7720.For all other Lamont questions, please call 303-871-6400.

He earned a BM from the Eastman School of Music in New York and an MMus from the Royal Academy of Music in London, United Kingdom. Tetreault is principal timpanist of the Central City Opera Orchestra, Boulder Chamber Orchestra and Fort Collins Symphony; principal percussionist of the Boulder Philharmonic and former assistant principal percussionist of the Colorado Springs Philharmonic. events.title: Reed Knife Sharpening Workshop with Tom Blodgett
events.description: Back to Event Listing // If the user came from a different site or has referrer clicking the back button will take them to the default listing page if (document.referrer === '' || document.referrer.search('du.edu') === -1) { document.querySelector('.sub-menu__back-link').onclick = function(e) { window.location.href = 'https://www.du.edu/calendar'; } } March 7 10:00am - 11:30am Newman Center for the Performing Arts, Room 209, Classroom Free admissionThis event will not be livestreamedB. Thomas (Tom) Blodgett sharpening journey began in 2000. What started as a personal pursuit soon became a professional passion, leading to the founding of Jende Industries in 2004. Today, with established websites in the USA, Australia and Taiwan, Jende Industries has grown into one of the leading global providers of custom-sized sharpening stones, strops, emulsions, reed knives, tools, and chef knife rolls.Driven by a constant desire to refine craft, Tom continue to experiment and explore new sharpening techniques — sometimes succeeding, sometimes learning. For those beginning their own sharpening journey, the insights shared here may help you navigate the many twists, turns, and bits of conflicting advice you’ll encounter, and hopefully save you from a few of the mistakes he has already made along the way. Learn more. For ticket questions, please contact the Newman Center Box Office at 303-871-7720.For all other Lamont questions, please call 303-871-6400.

events.title: Lamont Wind Ensemble
events.description: Back to Event Listing // If the user came from a different site or has referrer clicking the back button will take them to the default listing page if (document.referrer === '' || document.referrer.search('du.edu') === -1) { document.querySelector('.sub-menu__back-link').onclick = function(e) { window.location.href = 'https://www.du.edu/calendar'; } } March 11 7:30pm - 9:30pm Newman Center for the Performing Arts, Gates Concert Hall Purchase tickets: $5 for reserved parterre seats, or FREE general admission ($3 fee to purchase online)LivestreamProgram to comeFree parking: Register hereThe Lamont Wind Ensemble spotlights the energy and innovation of contemporary music in “Modern Movements.” From the driving rhythms of Michael Gandolfi’s Flourishes and Meditations to the sultry grooves of John Mackey’s Strange Humors—performed with guest artists Denver Dance—this program pulses with vitality. events.title: Guest artist recital: Mendelssohn Trio
events.description: Back to Event Listing // If the user came from a different site or has referrer clicking the back button will take them to the default listing page if (document.referrer === '' || document.referrer.search('du.edu') === -1) { document.querySelector('.sub-menu__back-link').onclick = function(e) { window.location.href = 'https://www.du.edu/calendar'; } } March 11 7:30pm - 9:30pm Newman Center for the Performing Arts, Hamilton Hall Free admissionThis event will not be livestreamedThe Mendelssohn Trio (Erik Peterson, violin; Barbara Thiem, cello; Bryan Wallick, piano) was formed in 1988 and is named for Thiem's great grandfather Franz von Mendelssohn, a nephew of Felix Mendelssohn Bartholdy and an important supporter of artists and musicians in the Berlin of the early 20th century. The trio is in residence at Colorado State University where they give frequent concerts and work with the students on chamber music projects.In addition to concerts in the US the trio has performed on five tours to several European countries including Germany, Austria and Switzerland. In 2012 their concerts included an appearance at the International Mendelssohn Festival in Berlin. The plans for a tour in May 2026 include a program of women composers from the US and Europe. Among other venues they will be playing at the Mendelssohn Museum in Berlin and the Mendelsohn Haus in Leipzig as well as for the Maria-Anna-Mozart-Gesellschaft in Salzburg.While performing the standard repertoire of the 18th and 19th centuries the trio has made an effort to introduce its audiences to works by 20th century composers such as the Americans Muczynski, Copland and Susanne Sorkin, as well as Europeans Martinu, Bridge, Martin and Bloch, and the little known French composer Theodore Gouvy (1819-1898). This year's program is presenting Amy Beach, Cecile Chaminade, Lera Auerbach and Jennifer Higdon. Learn more. For ticket questions, please contact the Newman Center Box Office at 303-871-7720.For all other Lamont questions, please call 303-871-6400.

events.title: Lamont Winter Colloquium
events.description: Back to Event Listing // If the user came from a different site or has referrer clicking the back button will take them to the default listing page if (document.referrer === '' || document.referrer.search('du.edu') === -1) { document.querySelector('.sub-menu__back-link').onclick = function(e) { window.location.href = 'https://www.du.edu/calendar'; } } March 13 2:30pm - 4:00pm Newman Center for the Performing Arts, Room 229, Classroom Free admissionThis event will not be livestreamedLamont Colloquium is a forum for Lamont students, faculty, and staff to present scholarly projects they have developed in the course of their studies, and share them with the broader Lamont community. It is designed to simulate the experience of presenting at an academic conference. The format for each speaker is a 20-minute scripted presentation, followed by ten minutes for questions. Topics may be anything connected with music: Past presentations have been on topics as diverse as representation of Georgian music in Sid Meier’s Civilization, metric structures in Spanish vihuela music, and issues in twentieth-century Balinese gamelan angklung. Students often use this experience as a stepping-stone to presenting at a regional chapter conference like SMT/AMS/SEM. For ticket questions, please contact the Newman Center Box Office at 303-871-7720.For all other Lamont questions, please call 303-871-6400.

events.title: Guest Recital: Lynnli Wang, carillon
events.description: Back to Event Listing // If the user came from a different site or has referrer clicking the back button will take them to the default listing page if (document.referrer === '' || document.referrer.search('du.edu') === -1) { document.querySelector('.sub-menu__back-link').onclick = function(e) { window.location.href = 'https://www.du.edu/calendar'; } } April 12 5:00pm - 6:00pm Williams Carillon, Ritchie Center LawnFree admission, no ticket requiredThis performance will not be livestreamedThis recital may be enjoyed from the south courtyard next to the Ritchie Center gym off Asbury Avenue. Attendees are invited to tour the Williams Carillon after the performance.Free parking is available on High Street and hourly paid parking is in the Cable Center parking lot. Based in New York City, Lynnli Wang is a performer, teacher, and diversity and inclusion arts advocate. She currently serves as the carillonneur of the Riverside Church, where she plays its 100+ ton, 74-bell carillon. Previously, she taught at Indiana University’s (IU) Jacobs School of Music as an Associate Instructor in Carillon, where she built and taught a vibrant carillon studio, dedicated two newly renovated carillons, performed weekly recitals, collaborated with student composers, commissioned a trio of new works by female composers, and authored her second children’s book on carillons. In recognition of her dedication to making carillon accessible and exciting to performers, listeners, and composers alike, Lynnli was awarded the IU New Music Performance Award, the IU Lieber Memorial Teaching Award, the Midwestern Association of Graduate Schools Teaching Award, and one of The Diapason’s 20 Under 30 awards.Lynnli’s carillon journey began at Yale in 2011, where she completed her undergraduate studies in literature. She passed the Guild of Carillonneurs in North America (GCNA) carillonneur exam in 2014; since then, she has concertized regularly across the U.S. To advance the art of carillon, Lynnli has served on the board of the GCNA and various committees, including the Emerging Artist Grant and the Sally Slade Warner Arrangements & Transcriptions Competition Jury. For ticket questions, please contact the Newman Center Box Office at 303-871-7720.For all other Lamont questions, please call 303-871-6400.

Trumpet Professor Brian Neal takes the spotlight in the world premiere wind ensemble arrangement of his Emmy Award–winning Concertante, paired with Joseph Turrin’s Evensong for trumpet and winds. With its blend of virtuosity, grandeur, and fresh compositional voices, this concert celebrates the artistry and innovation driving today’s wind music. events.title: Guest Recital: Annie Gao, carillon
events.description: Back to Event Listing // If the user came from a different site or has referrer clicking the back button will take them to the default listing page if (document.referrer === '' || document.referrer.search('du.edu') === -1) { document.querySelector('.sub-menu__back-link').onclick = function(e) { window.location.href = 'https://www.du.edu/calendar'; } } May 25 5:00pm - 6:00pm Williams Carillon, Ritchie Center LawnFree admission, no ticket requiredThis performance will not be livestreamedThis recital may be enjoyed from the south courtyard next to the Ritchie Center gym off Asbury Avenue. Attendees are invited to tour the Williams Carillon after the performance.Free parking is available on High Street and hourly paid parking is in the Cable Center parking lot.Annie Gao is a carillonist and software engineer from the sunny suburbs of Southern California. She first met the carillon in 2017 as a member of the Yale University Guild of Carillonneurs, completed her GCNA Carillonneur exam in 2020, and graduated from Yale in 2021 with a combined B.S./M.S. in Computer Science. She then had the tremendous privilege of continuing her carillon studies internationally with Geert D’hollander, Boudewijn Zwart, and Eddy Marien, and along the way, was selected for the quinquennial Kors Monster Festival Performance Nomination (2024), as well as The Diapason's "20 Under 30" list (2025). She has taught carillon performance masterclasses all over the United States, and currently serves the GCNA as chair of the Emerging Artists Grant committee and jury member of the Carillonneur Exam. In her free time, Annie enjoys competitive ping pong, puzzles and brainteasers, good reads, and opportunities to explore and improve her fascinatingly bad sense of direction. For ticket questions, please contact the Newman Center Box Office at 303-871-7720.For all other Lamont questions, please call 303-871-6400.

events.title: Guest Recital: Carol Jickling Lens, carillon
events.description: Back to Event Listing // If the user came from a different site or has referrer clicking the back button will take them to the default listing page if (document.referrer === '' || document.referrer.search('du.edu') === -1) { document.querySelector('.sub-menu__back-link').onclick = function(e) { window.location.href = 'https://www.du.edu/calendar'; } } July 5 5:00pm - 6:00pm Williams Carillon, Ritchie Center LawnFree admission, no ticket requiredThis performance will not be livestreamedThis recital may be enjoyed from the south courtyard next to the Ritchie Center gym off Asbury Avenue. Attendees are invited to tour the Williams Carillon after the performance.Free parking is available on High Street and hourly paid parking is in the Cable Center parking lot. Carol Jickling Lens began her carillon studies in 8th grade at Christ Church Cranbrook. She continued at the University of Michigan and The Netherlands Carillon School in Amersfoort, where she studied with Leen ‘t Hart, and earned both the Practical and End Diplomas. Carol became Carillonneur member of the GCNA in 1971 and was a judge and then chair of the Exam Committee for many years, sat on the Board from 1996-99 and was reelected in 2017. Carol and her family have lived in England, Ghana, New Orleans, Houston and Libya. She was appointed University Carillonneur at The University of Denver on January 5, 2010 and held the position until 2022. For ticket questions, please contact the Newman Center Box Office at 303-871-7720.For all other Lamont questions, please call 303-871-6400.
